You are doing an ADVERSARIAL code review of the metals-core subsystem (metals/oil/crypto swing loops, grid-fisher market-maker, fishing tools) of a quantitative trading system at Q:\finance-analyzer. Sandbox: read-only.

In-scope files (read these and ONLY these):
- data/metals_loop.py
- data/crypto_loop.py
- data/oil_loop.py
- portfolio/grid_fisher.py
- portfolio/grid_fisher_config.py

- portfolio/elongir/   (recurse)
- portfolio/golddigger/ (recurse)

Project rules:
- Metals loop is a SEPARATE process from main Layer 1 loop. 60s cycle with embedded 10s silver fast-tick.
- Avanza commodity warrants trade 08:15-21:55 CET (NOT 17:25). Regular Swedish equities 09:00-17:25 CET.
- Grid fisher: 2-tier buy ladders (1200 SEK/leg, 6500 SEK global cap), with-signal direction only, EOD-flat.
- Use forward slashes in paths. Atomic I/O via file_utils.
- Stop-loss via /_api/trading/stoploss/new only.
- Local LLM (Ministral-8B / Chronos-2 / Qwen3-8B) runs through GPU lock.
- Live prices first — silver warrants are volatile, never trade on stale data.
- Never re-enter same warrant on signal flip without bounce confirmation (per memory rules).

Adversarial focus:
1. Trading-hours mismatch — commodity warrants vs equities (DST-aware).
2. Order races between metals loop and grid fisher (both trading XAG/XAU).
3. EOD-flat logic: cutoff time wrong, partial-fill leg not closed.
4. Stop-loss API misuse (Mar 3 instant-fill incident repeat).
5. MINI warrant barrier proximity check before placing stops.
6. Order lock / process lock contention with main loop and golddigger.
7. Silent failure on Avanza session expiry (BankID 24h re-auth) — silent retry vs surface.
8. Grid sizing: cap not enforced on cumulative open exposure, only per-leg.
9. Precompute staleness: data fetched once at boot then reused all day.
10. fin_snipe/fin_fish: hardcoded paths, missing tz, wrong rounding for Swedish krona vs USD.
11. ORB predictor look-ahead bias.

Output format — strict markdown:

1560:def is_market_hours():
1561-    """Check if Avanza commodity warrant market is open (Mon-Fri 08:15-21:55 Stockholm time).

2050:def _eod_sell_fishing_positions(page):
2051-    """Sell all active fishing positions at end of day (21:50 CET).
2052-
2053-    Fishing positions are intraday only — never hold overnight.
2054:    Uses emergency_sell for immediate market execution.
2055-    """
2056:    sold = []
2057-    for key, pos in POSITIONS.items():
2058-        if not pos.get("active"):
2059-            continue
2060-        ob_id = pos.get("ob_id", "")
2061-        # Check _fishing flag OR ob_id in FISHING_OB_IDS (handles restart race)
2062-        if not pos.get("_fishing") and ob_id not in FISHING_OB_IDS:
2063-            continue
2064-        try:
2065-            price_data = fetch_price(page, ob_id, pos.get("api_type", "certificate"))
2066-            bid = (price_data or {}).get("bid", 0)
2067-            if bid > 0:
2068-                log(f"[fish-eod] Selling {key} at bid={bid} (EOD 21:50)")
2069:                emergency_sell(page, key, pos, bid)
2070-                sold.append(f"{key} @ {bid}")
2071-            else:
2072-                log(f"[fish-eod] No bid for {key}, attempting emergency sell at 0")
2073:                emergency_sell(page, key, pos, 0)
2074-                sold.append(f"{key} @ market")
2075-        except Exception as e:
2076-            log(f"[fish-eod] Error selling {key}: {e}")
2077-    if sold:
2078-        send_telegram(
2079-            "*FISH EOD SELL*\n"
2080-            + "\n".join(f"  {s}" for s in sold)
2081-            + "\nIntraday fishing positions closed for the day."
2082-        )
2083-    return sold

Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-1-"""Grid market-maker orchestrator for leveraged warrants.
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-2-
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-3-Holds the runtime state of a multi-tier limit ladder per (instrument)
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-4-plus the lifecycle around it:
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-5-
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-6-  STARTUP   load state -> reconcile against live Avanza -> resume or reset
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-7-  TICK      check signal -> arm/cancel ladder -> place tiers -> detect fills
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py:8:            -> rotate filled buys into sell+stop -> log -> sweep at EOD
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-9-  SHUTDOWN  persist state
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-10-
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-11-This file contains the state-machine + persistence layer. Order placement
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-12-and Avanza side effects land in a subsequent batch — the public surface
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-13-here is split so the state code is unit-testable in isolation.
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-14-
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-15-State is held in ``data/grid_fisher_state.json`` (atomic JSON via
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-16-``file_utils``) and every transition is appended to
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-17-``data/grid_fisher_decisions.jsonl``. Both are read by the dashboard for
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-18-ops visibility.
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-19-"""

Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py:262:    """Minutes until the grid fisher's day-end cutoff in Europe/Stockholm.
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-263-
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-264-    Returns a non-negative float; if the cutoff has already passed for the
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-265-    day, returns the minutes-until-tomorrow's cutoff (so the caller can
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py:266:    detect with ``< EOD_SWEEP_MINUTES_BEFORE`` only during the active
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-267-    window). Returns ``float("inf")`` if zoneinfo is unavailable so the
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py:268:    caller never triggers EOD on the failure path.
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-269-    """
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-270-    try:
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-271-        import zoneinfo  # noqa: PLC0415 — optional import to keep startup cheap
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-272-    except ImportError:
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-273-        return float("inf")

Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-280-    cutoff = local.replace(

Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-282-    )
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-283-    if cutoff <= local:

Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-285-    delta = cutoff - local

148-    @classmethod
149-    def from_dict(cls, d: dict[str, Any]) -> "InstrumentState":

152-            ticker=str(d["ticker"]),
153-            cert_name=str(d.get("cert_name", "")),
154-            active_direction=d.get("active_direction"),

157-            stop_loss_id=d.get("stop_loss_id"),
158-            stop_loss_price=d.get("stop_loss_price"),

162-            fills_this_session=int(d.get("fills_this_session", 0) or 0),
163-            consecutive_losses=int(d.get("consecutive_losses", 0) or 0),
164-            cooldown_until=d.get("cooldown_until"),
165-            last_direction_flip_ts=d.get("last_direction_flip_ts"),
166-        )

168-    # ---- queries -----------------------------------------------------------
169-
170:    def armed_buy_tiers(self) -> list[TierOrder]:
171:        return [o for o in self.buy_ladder if o.status == ORDER_ARMED]
172-
173-    def armed_sell_tiers(self) -> list[TierOrder]:
174:        return [o for o in self.sell_ladder if o.status == ORDER_ARMED]
175-
176:    def planned_notional_sek(self) -> float:
177-        """SEK at risk = armed buys (notional) + inventory @ avg entry."""
178:        armed = sum(o.price * o.qty for o in self.armed_buy_tiers())
179:        held = self.inventory_units * self.avg_entry_price
180-        return armed + held
181-
182:    def hit_per_instrument_cap(self) -> bool:
183:        return self.planned_notional_sek() >= GRID_PER_INSTRUMENT_MAX_SEK
184-
185-    def session_loss_breached(self) -> bool:
186-        return self.session_pnl_sek <= -abs(GRID_PER_SESSION_LOSS_LIMIT_SEK)
187-
188-    def in_cooldown(self, now_iso: Optional[str] = None) -> bool:
189-        if not self.cooldown_until:
190-            return False
191-        now_iso = now_iso or _utcnow_iso()
192-        return now_iso < self.cooldown_until

--
485-    fill_ts: Optional[str] = None,
486-) -> Optional[TierOrder]:
487-    """Mark a buy or sell tier as FILLED and update inventory + P&L.
488-
489-    Returns the updated ``TierOrder`` or ``None`` if no matching armed
490-    tier was found.
491-    """
492-    if side not in ("buy", "sell"):
493-        raise ValueError(f"side must be 'buy' or 'sell', got {side!r}")
494-    ladder = inst.buy_ladder if side == "buy" else inst.sell_ladder
495-    target: Optional[TierOrder] = None
496-    for o in ladder:
497:        if o.tier == tier_idx and o.status == ORDER_ARMED:
498-            target = o
499-            break
500-    if target is None:
501-        return None
502-
503-    target.status = ORDER_FILLED
504-    target.fill_ts = fill_ts or _utcnow_iso()
505-    target.fill_price = fill_price
506-    inst.fills_this_session += 1
507-
508-    if side == "buy":
509-        # Weighted-average entry
510:        prev_units = inst.inventory_units
511:        prev_avg = inst.avg_entry_price
512-        new_units = prev_units + target.qty
513-        if new_units > 0:
514:            inst.avg_entry_price = (
515-                (prev_avg * prev_units) + (fill_price * target.qty)
516-            ) / new_units
517:        inst.inventory_units = new_units
518-    else:  # sell
519-        # Realise P&L on the qty exiting
520:        pnl_per_unit = fill_price - inst.avg_entry_price
521-        realised = pnl_per_unit * target.qty
522-        inst.session_pnl_sek += realised
523:        inst.inventory_units = max(0, inst.inventory_units - target.qty)
524:        if inst.inventory_units == 0:
525:            inst.avg_entry_price = 0.0
526-        if realised < 0:
527-            inst.consecutive_losses += 1
528-        else:
529-            inst.consecutive_losses = 0
530-
531-    return target
532-

534-def cancel_buy_tier(
535-    inst: InstrumentState,
536-    tier_idx: int,
537-) -> Optional[TierOrder]:

546-def prune_terminal_orders(inst: InstrumentState) -> None:
547-    """Drop FILLED/CANCELLED/REJECTED tiers from in-memory ladders.
548-
549-    Persisted decision log retains them. Pruning keeps the in-memory
550-    ladder bounded so each tick only sees ARMED orders.
551-    """
552:    inst.buy_ladder = [o for o in inst.buy_ladder if o.status == ORDER_ARMED]
553:    inst.sell_ladder = [o for o in inst.sell_ladder if o.status == ORDER_ARMED]

561:def global_planned_notional(state: GridFisherState) -> float:
562:    """Sum planned notional across every instrument (armed buys +
563-    inventory at avg entry). Used by the global-cap gate."""
564:    return sum(inst.planned_notional_sek()
565-               for inst in state.by_instrument.values())

568-def should_halt_global(state: GridFisherState) -> Optional[str]:
569-    """Return a halt reason string if the global drawdown breaks a limit,
570-    else None. Caller flips ``state.halted = True`` and logs.
571-
572-    Currently uses session-wide P&L; richer drawdown tracking lives in
573-    ``risk_management.py`` and is wired in via a later batch.
574-    """
575-    # Per-session global loss limit = sum of per-instrument limits.
576-    threshold = -abs(GRID_PER_SESSION_LOSS_LIMIT_SEK) * max(
--
614-    open_order_ids: set[str],
615-    positions: list[dict[str, Any]],
616-) -> ReconcileResult:
617-    """Diff in-memory state against live Avanza state.
618-
619-    For each ARMED tier whose order_id is missing from ``open_order_ids``:
620-      * if the live position covers the expected delta -> mark FILLED at
621-        the tier's limit price (best estimate; actual fill price may be
622-        slightly better)
623-      * else -> mark CANCELLED (likely cancelled externally)
624-
625-    Also reports inventory drift where the live volume disagrees with
626:    the cached ``inventory_units`` for the instrument; the caller may
627-    use this to align state without erroneously double-counting fills.
628-
629-    Returns a ``ReconcileResult`` listing every transition for logging.
630-    """
631-    res = ReconcileResult()
632-    for ob_id, inst in state.by_instrument.items():
633-        live_vol = _position_volume_for(positions, ob_id)
634-
635-        for tier in list(inst.buy_ladder):
636:            if tier.status != ORDER_ARMED:
637-                continue
638-            if tier.order_id and tier.order_id in open_order_ids:
639-                continue
640-            # Order is missing from live. Distinguish full fill / partial
641-            # fill / cancel by the live position delta vs cached
642-            # inventory (state already accounts for any earlier tiers
643-            # processed this loop because record_fill mutates inst).
644:            delta = live_vol - inst.inventory_units
645-            if delta >= tier.qty:
646-                record_fill(inst, tier.tier, tier.price, side="buy")
647-                res.filled_buys.append((ob_id, tier.tier, tier.price))
648-            elif delta > 0:
649-                # Partial fill — the order filled `delta` units before
650-                # being cancelled (or capped by quote-side liquidity).
651-                # Record the fill with reduced qty so inventory accounting
652-                # stays honest, and log the drift so an operator can spot
653-                # repeated partials and tune tier sizing.
654-                original_qty = tier.qty
655-                tier.qty = int(delta)
656-                record_fill(inst, tier.tier, tier.price, side="buy")
657-                res.filled_buys.append((ob_id, tier.tier, tier.price))
658-                res.inventory_drift.append((ob_id, original_qty, int(delta)))
659-            else:
660-                cancel_buy_tier(inst, tier.tier)
661-                res.cancelled_buys.append((ob_id, tier.tier))
662-
663-        for tier in list(inst.sell_ladder):
664:            if tier.status != ORDER_ARMED:
665-                continue
666-            if tier.order_id and tier.order_id in open_order_ids:
667-                continue
668:            inventory_drop = inst.inventory_units - live_vol
669-            if inventory_drop >= tier.qty:
670-                record_fill(inst, tier.tier, tier.price, side="sell")
671-                res.filled_sells.append((ob_id, tier.tier, tier.price))
672-            elif inventory_drop > 0:
673-                original_qty = tier.qty
674-                tier.qty = int(inventory_drop)
675-                record_fill(inst, tier.tier, tier.price, side="sell")
676-                res.filled_sells.append((ob_id, tier.tier, tier.price))
677-                res.inventory_drift.append(
678-                    (ob_id, original_qty, int(inventory_drop))
679-                )
680-            else:
681-                # Mark sell tier as cancelled in-place — there is no
682-                # dedicated helper because sells are cleared via rotation.
683-                tier.status = ORDER_CANCELLED
684-                res.cancelled_sells.append((ob_id, tier.tier))
685-
686-        # Final drift check — only used for logging; the caller decides
687-        # whether to forcibly align (we do not auto-rewrite inventory).
688:        if live_vol != inst.inventory_units:
689-            res.inventory_drift.append(
690:                (ob_id, inst.inventory_units, live_vol)
691-            )
692-    return res

699-
700-class GridFisher:
701-    """High-level driver — owns state + session + signal/quote callables.
702-
--
863-    def cancel_armed_buys(self, inst: InstrumentState) -> int:
864-        """Cancel every ARMED buy tier on *inst*. Returns count cancelled.
865-
866-        Avanza's cancel endpoint returns a JSON body even on rejection
867-        (no exception). Only marks the tier ``CANCELLED`` after a
868-        confirmed ``orderRequestStatus == "SUCCESS"`` response, so a
869-        broker-side rejection leaves the order ARMED in state and the
870-        next tick's reconcile picks it up correctly — preventing
871-        duplicate placements on top of a still-resting buy.
872-        """
873-        n = 0
874-        for tier in list(inst.buy_ladder):
875:            if tier.status != ORDER_ARMED:
876-                continue
877-            if not tier.order_id:
878-                # Never accepted by Avanza in the first place — just drop.
879-                cancel_buy_tier(inst, tier.tier)
880-                n += 1

798-
799-    def _safe_session_call(self, fn, *args, default=None, **kwargs):
800-        """Invoke an Avanza session method from a persistent worker thread.
801-
802-        The metals loop runs Playwright async context for its swing-trader
803-        page; the REST avanza_session module that grid_fisher uses internally
804-        spins up its own sync_playwright client. Calling sync Playwright APIs
805-        from a thread that has a running asyncio event loop raises
806-        "Playwright Sync API inside the asyncio loop". Spawning a fresh
807:        worker per call (e.g. with a per-call ThreadPoolExecutor) fixes
808-        that but breaks the *next* call: the avanza_session module caches
809-        its Playwright context to the FIRST thread that initialised it, so
810-        when a later call lands in a different worker thread it raises
811-        "cannot switch to a different thread (which happens to have exited)".
812-        Solution: one long-lived worker thread for the lifetime of this
813-        GridFisher — all session calls land on the same thread and the
814-        cached Playwright context stays bound to it.
815-
816-        On timeout or worker exception, ``default`` is returned and the
817-        failure is logged via the journal.
818-        """
819-        import concurrent.futures
820-
821-        # Lazily create the persistent single-worker executor. Held on
822-        # the instance so process shutdown cleans it up via __del__.
823-        if getattr(self, "_session_executor", None) is None:
824:            self._session_executor = concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(
825:                max_workers=1, thread_name_prefix="grid-fisher-session",
826-            )
827-
828-        def _runner():
829-            return fn(*args, **kwargs)
830-
831-        future = self._session_executor.submit(_runner)
832-        try:
833-            return future.result(timeout=30)
834-        except concurrent.futures.TimeoutError:
835:            self._log("session_call_timeout",
836-                      method=getattr(fn, "__name__", repr(fn)))
837:            return default
838-        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
839:            self._log("session_call_error",
840-                      method=getattr(fn, "__name__", repr(fn)),
841-                      error=str(exc))
842:            return default
843-
844-    def __del__(self):
845-        # Best-effort shutdown of the worker thread on GC. The metals
846-        # loop holds the GridFisher for its lifetime so this only runs
847-        # at process exit.
848-        executor = getattr(self, "_session_executor", None)
849-        if executor is not None:
850-            try:
851-                executor.shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)
852-            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
853-                pass
854-

Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-1293-
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-1294-            # Each instrument has a FIXED natural direction from the
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-1295-            # catalog (BULL=LONG, BEAR=SHORT) baked in at seed time.
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-1296-            # If the signal points the OTHER way, this instrument is on
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-1297-            # the wrong side of the market — cancel any armed buys
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-1298-            # (don't keep fishing into a moving signal) and skip the
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-1299-            # placement step. Existing inventory + sell ladder + stop
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-1300-            # are left alone so the original position can exit on its
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-1301-            # own terms.
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py:1302:            if inst.active_direction != direction:
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-1303-                cancelled = self.cancel_armed_buys(inst)
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-1304-                report["cancels"] += cancelled
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-1305-                instr_report["skip"] = "signal_direction_mismatch"
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-1306-                instr_report["cancelled"] = cancelled
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-1307-                report["instruments"][ob_id] = instr_report
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-1308-                continue
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-1309-
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py-1310-            # Cooldown check — set after we last cancelled buys on this
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/grid_fisher.py:1311:            # instrument (signal flipped away then back too quickly).

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1-"""
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-2-Unified Market Monitoring Loop v10 (Layer 1 — Autonomous).
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-3-Runs every 60s, fully autonomous without Claude Code dependency.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:4:Tracks: XAG/XAU (Binance FAPI), BTC/ETH (Binance SPOT), MSTR (Yahoo).
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-5-Core features: probability-focused Telegram, momentum-aware trailing stops,
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-6-auto-detect holdings, per-signal accuracy, crypto Fear & Greed, on-chain metrics.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-7-
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-8-v10: Silver fast-tick monitor merged from silver_monitor.py — 10-second price
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-9-checks with instant threshold alerts (-3% to -12.5%) and 3-minute velocity
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-10-flush detection.  Replaces the standalone silver_monitor.py process.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-11-
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-12-Features:
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-13-- Silver fast-tick: 10s price checks during 60s cycle sleep (threshold + velocity alerts)
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-14-- Tiered Claude invocation (Haiku/Sonnet, no Opus)
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-15-- Local LLM inference (Ministral-8B + Chronos for all tracked symbols)
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-16-- Monte Carlo VaR for leveraged warrants
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:17:- Trade guards (cooldowns, session limits, loss escalation)
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-18-- Drawdown circuit breaker (-15% emergency liquidation)
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-19-- Multi-level stop-loss (L1 warn / L2 alert / L3 emergency auto-sell)
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:20:- Short instrument tracking (BEAR SILVER X5)
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-21-- Time server (timeapi.io) for accurate CET
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-22-- Daily range analysis (historical percentiles + intraday assessment)
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-23-- Spike catcher (limit sell orders before US open)
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-24-- Invocation logging (tier/model/trigger tracking)
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-25-- Crypto data: Fear & Greed, CryptoCompare news, on-chain (MVRV/SOPR)
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-26-- MSTR-BTC NAV premium tracking
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-27-
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-28-Run: .venv/Scripts/python.exe data/metals_loop.py
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-29-"""

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-387-# --- CONFIG ---
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-388-CLAUDE_ENABLED = True         # 2026-04-15: re-enabled metals Layer 2 (user request)
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-389-CHECK_INTERVAL = 60           # target seconds between checks
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-390-TRIGGER_PRICE_MOVE = 5.0      # % move from last invocation to trigger (was 2.0)
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-391-TRIGGER_TRAILING = 8.0        # % drop from peak to trigger (was 3.0)
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-392-TRIGGER_PROFIT = 4.0          # % profit from entry to trigger
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-393-TRIGGER_STOP_NEAR = 5.0       # % from stop-loss to trigger
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-394-HEARTBEAT_CHECKS = 60         # invoke every N checks (~60 min at 60s)
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:395:# Per-tier cooldowns (seconds) — replaces flat MIN_INVOKE_INTERVAL
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-396-TIER_COOLDOWNS = {
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:397:    1: 120,    # Haiku: 2 min cooldown
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:398:    2: 600,    # Sonnet: 10 min cooldown
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:399:    3: 0,      # Critical: no cooldown (immediate)
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-400-}

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-405-SPIKE_PLACE_ET = (9, 15)      # place 15 min before NYSE open
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-406-SPIKE_OPEN_ET = (9, 30)       # NYSE regular session open
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-407-SPIKE_CANCEL_ET = (10, 30)    # cancel 1h after open if unfilled
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-408-SPIKE_PERCENTILE = 75          # P75 of daily open_to_high as target

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:479:# --- SILVER FAST-TICK MONITOR (merged from silver_monitor.py) ---
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-480-# Provides 10-second price checks with instant threshold alerts and velocity detection
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-481-# for active silver positions. Runs during _sleep_for_cycle() between main 60s cycles.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:482:SILVER_FAST_TICK_ENABLED = True
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:483:SILVER_FAST_TICK_INTERVAL = 10   # seconds between fast price checks
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:484:SILVER_ALERT_LEVELS = [
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-485-    (-3.0, "WARNING"),       # -14.3% warrant at ~4.76x
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-486-    (-5.0, "DANGER"),        # -23.8% warrant
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-487-    (-7.0, "HIGH RISK"),     # -33.3% warrant
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-488-    (-10.0, "CRITICAL"),     # -47.6% warrant
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-489-    (-12.5, "EMERGENCY"),    # -59.5% warrant
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-490-]
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:491:SILVER_VELOCITY_WINDOW = 18      # 18 × 10s = 3 min rolling window
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:492:SILVER_VELOCITY_ALERT_PCT = -0.8 # % drop threshold over the velocity window
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:493:SILVER_VELOCITY_TELEGRAM = True  # send Telegram on velocity alerts
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-494-
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-495-# --- ENTRY-SIDE FAST-TICK (upside-momentum detector, 2026-04-17) ---
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-496-# Mirror of the exit-side velocity alert but in the opposite direction and
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-497-# NOT gated on having an active position. When a breakout is detected we write
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-498-# a momentum candidate to ENTRY_MOMENTUM_STATE_FILE; metals_swing_trader reads
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-499-# this file and relaxes the usual MIN_BUY_CONFIDENCE/MIN_BUY_VOTERS gates when
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-500-# a fresh candidate matches the ticker and direction.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-501-#
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-502-# Motivation: the 2026-04-17 silver rip moved +1.3% in 20 min on volume ratio
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-503-# 2.2x while the swing trader's snapshot-based gates required
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-504-# MIN_BUY_CONFIDENCE (was 0.60, since 2026-05-04 0.56) AND 2-consecutive
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-505-# regime-confirm checks. Those thresholds are correct for range entries but
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-506-# silently reject breakout entries that move faster than the confirmation cadence.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:507:SILVER_ENTRY_FAST_TICK_ENABLED = True
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:508:SILVER_ENTRY_VELOCITY_WINDOW = 18          # 18 × 10s = 3 min (matches exit window)
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:509:SILVER_ENTRY_VELOCITY_ALERT_PCT = 0.8      # +% rise required over the window
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:510:SILVER_ENTRY_MIN_RVOL = 1.5                # require volume ratio ≥ this (vol confirm)
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:511:SILVER_ENTRY_DEDUP_WINDOW_SEC = 300        # one trigger per 5 min max
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:512:GOLD_ENTRY_FAST_TICK_ENABLED = True
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:513:GOLD_ENTRY_VELOCITY_WINDOW = 18            # same 3-min window
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:514:GOLD_ENTRY_VELOCITY_ALERT_PCT = 0.4        # gold's typical range is ~half of silver's
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:515:GOLD_ENTRY_MIN_RVOL = 1.5
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:516:GOLD_ENTRY_DEDUP_WINDOW_SEC = 300
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-517-ENTRY_MOMENTUM_STATE_FILE = os.path.join("data", "metals_momentum_state.json")

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-807-# Timeline:
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-808-#   2026-04-07: first live test, 6 integration bugs cost 590 SEK.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-809-#   2026-04-09: all 6 bugs fixed; position reconciliation added.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-810-#   2026-04-15: subsequent session lost 12,257 SEK across 12 consecutive
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-811-#               trades with the integration working correctly.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-812-#
--
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-827-#      signal regime.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-828-#   2. Documented justification in docs/plans/<date>-fish-engine-revival.md.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-829-#   3. Removal of both FISH_ENGINE_ENABLED=False AND _assert_fish_engine_allowed.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-830-#
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-831-# The two-step requirement (flag + assertion removal) is intentional: a
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-832-# single-line change cannot reactivate a known-losing strategy.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-833-FISH_ENGINE_ENABLED = False
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-834-
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-835-

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:839:    If someone flips ``FISH_ENGINE_ENABLED = True`` without also removing
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-840-    this assertion and the call sites below, the first execution path
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-841-    raises loudly instead of silently activating a losing strategy.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-842-
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-843-    Kept as a function (not a module-level assert) so it is testable and
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-844-    so future operators who intentionally revive the engine have to make
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-845-    a deliberate, auditable change (delete the function AND remove the
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-846-    call at every use site). See the FISH_ENGINE_ENABLED declaration for
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-847-    the full revival checklist.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-848-    """
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-849-    if FISH_ENGINE_ENABLED:
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-850-        raise RuntimeError(
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-851-            "Fish engine deprecated 2026-04-17 — see FISH_ENGINE_ENABLED "

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1131-# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1132-# Entry-side fast-tick (2026-04-17 upside-momentum detector)
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1133-#
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1134-# Mirror of the exit-side silver fast-tick but direction-reversed and
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1135-# position-agnostic. Designed to catch coordinated risk-on bursts (e.g. BTC
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1136-# squeeze → silver follows) that the snapshot-based swing trader gates miss
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1137-# because the regime/voter confirm takes 2 cycles while the move is 20 min
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1138-# long. When a velocity breakout is detected we write a momentum candidate
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1139-# to ENTRY_MOMENTUM_STATE_FILE; metals_swing_trader consumes this file.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1140-# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
--
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1157-    ticker: str,
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1158-    direction: str,

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1163-) -> None:
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1164-    """Atomically write a momentum candidate for ticker.

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1166-    Preserves existing entries for other tickers so BTC candidates (added
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1167-    later) don't stomp silver candidates and vice versa. Schema:
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1168-
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:1169:      {"XAG-USD": {"direction": "LONG", "velocity_pct": 0.92, ...}}

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1178-        "consumed_at": None,

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1192-
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1193-def _fetch_rvol(ticker: str) -> float:
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1194-    """Fetch relative volume ratio (last 1h volume / 24h avg) from Binance FAPI.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1195-
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1196-    Returns 1.0 on any error — neutral (neither confirms nor rejects momentum).
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1197-    A dedicated RVOL fetch is unavoidable because the main loop's per-cycle
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1198-    volume_ratio is computed from the signal engine and not exposed in any
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1199-    shared module state that the fast-tick can reach without a cycle hit.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1200-    """
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1201-    try:
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1202-        # Use the 24h klines endpoint: last 1h volume vs. average of the
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1203-        # preceding 24 hours. Lightweight — single request, one response.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:1204:        symbol = "XAGUSDT" if ticker == "XAG-USD" else "XAUUSDT"
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1205-        r = requests.get(
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1206-            f"{BINANCE_FAPI_TICKER.rsplit('/', 1)[0]}/klines?"
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1207-            f"symbol={symbol}&interval=1h&limit=25",
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1208-            timeout=5,
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1209-        )
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1210-        if r.status_code != 200:
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1211-            return 1.0
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1212-        bars = r.json()
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1213-        if not bars or len(bars) < 2:
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1214-            return 1.0
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1215-        # Kline index 5 is volume (base asset). Last bar is the currently
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1216-        # forming hour; compare its volume against the mean of the previous
--
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1233-def _entry_fast_tick(
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1234-    ticker: str,
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1235-    fetch_fn,
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1236-    prices_deque: deque,
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1237-    threshold_pct: float,
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1238-    min_rvol: float,
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1239-    dedup_sec: int,
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1240-    ttl_sec: int = 300,
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1241-) -> None:
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1242-    """Generic entry-side fast-tick: detect positive velocity breakouts.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1243-
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1244-    Mirror of ``_silver_fast_tick`` but direction-reversed. Runs every
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:1245:    SILVER_FAST_TICK_INTERVAL seconds regardless of whether the user holds a
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1246-    position — the point is to trigger entry decisions.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1247-
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1248-    Steps:
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1249-      1. Fetch live price; append to rolling deque.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1250-      2. When deque is full, compute velocity = (newest - oldest) / oldest * 100.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1251-      3. If velocity >= threshold_pct AND rvol >= min_rvol AND dedup elapsed,
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1252-         write a momentum candidate and optionally send Telegram.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1253-
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1254-    The rvol gate is the primary false-positive defense — a velocity breakout
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1255-    on thin liquidity (e.g. off-hours spike) would write a candidate without
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1256-    it. RVOL is re-fetched per trigger (NOT per tick) because we only hit the
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1257-    rvol endpoint when velocity alone already qualifies — cheap.
--

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1296-    log(

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1409-    checks for significant drops from the entry reference price, and detects

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:1419:    # 2026-04-13: Opportunistic XAG microstructure snapshot at 10s cadence.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1420-    # Cycle-level _accumulate_orderbook_snapshots already runs this every
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1421-    # ~60s; the fast-tick adds 5x more snapshots while a position is held,
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1422-    # giving OFI/VPIN better resolution. Gated by _FAST_TICK_ORDERBOOK env
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1423-    # toggle for ops to disable if FAPI rate-limit pressure shows up.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1424-    # Best-effort: never blocks the price-check or alerts below.

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1755-# Independent log-throttle counter for the per-ticker fast-tick path.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1756-# Keeping it separate from _microstructure_persist_counter so the 1-in-30
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1757-# log gate inside _accumulate_orderbook_snapshot_for measures fast-tick
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1758-# call count, not cycle count. Without this, the cycle-level counter
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1759-# (incremented once per 60s) would freeze the fast-tick's % 30 check at
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1760-# whatever modulo it happens to land on, producing either no logs ever
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1761-# or every fast-tick logging unthrottled.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1762-_snapshot_for_call_counter = 0
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1763-# 2026-04-13: opt-in fast-tick microstructure accumulation. When True,
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:1764:# _silver_fast_tick triggers an XAG-only orderbook snapshot every 10s
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1765-# instead of just the once-per-cycle 60s snapshot. Improves OFI / VPIN
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1766-# resolution when a silver position is active. Env var lets ops toggle
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1767-# without code changes if Binance FAPI rate-limit pressure shows up.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1768-_FAST_TICK_ORDERBOOK = (
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1769-    __import__("os").environ.get("ORDERBOOK_FAST_TICK", "1") not in ("0", "false", "False", "")
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1770-)

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1773-    """Single-ticker orderbook snapshot accumulator.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1774-
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1775-    2026-04-13: factored out of _accumulate_orderbook_snapshots so the
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:1776:    silver fast-tick (10s cadence) can poll XAG depth without paying the
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:1777:    cost of XAU on every fast tick. Failures are logged at WARNING with
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1778-    1-in-30 throttling so a transient FAPI outage doesn't blow the log.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1779-
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1780-    Uses its own _snapshot_for_call_counter — NOT the cycle-level
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1781-    _microstructure_persist_counter — so the throttle gate measures actual
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1782-    helper invocations rather than cycle ticks.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1783-    """
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1784-    global _snapshot_for_call_counter
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1785-    if not _MICROSTRUCTURE_AVAILABLE:
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1786-        return
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1787-    try:
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1788-        depth = get_orderbook_depth(ticker, limit=20)
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1789-        if depth:

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-2389-    Normal: 5% below current bid
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-2390-    Momentum reversal: tighten to 3%
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-2391-    Accelerating decline: tighten to 2%
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-2392-
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-2393-    Returns: optimal stop price, trail_distance_pct used
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-2394-    """
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-2395-    # Determine underlying ticker for momentum

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-2398-
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-2399-    velocity = momentum["velocity_pct"]
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-2400-    acceleration = momentum["acceleration"]
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-2401-
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-2402-    # Base distance: 5%

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-2404-
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-2405-    # Tighten based on momentum
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-2406-    if velocity < -0.02 and acceleration < 0:
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-2407-        # Accelerating decline — tighten to 2%
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-2408-        trail_dist = TRAIL_TIGHTEN_ACCEL
--

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-3950-def _capture_stop_snapshot(ob_id):
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-3951-    """Read the live stop-loss inventory for ``ob_id`` BEFORE any cancel
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-3952-    side effect, returning ``(ok, snapshot)``.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-3953-
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-3954-    Captured snapshots are used as the rollback record for the
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-3955-    cancel-then-sell sequence. Snapshotting AFTER any cancel would miss
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-3956-    stops that were already removed at the broker, leaving the rollback
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-3957-    incomplete and the position naked on a failed sell.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-3958-
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-3959-    2026-04-13 (Bug 2a): retry once with a fresh Playwright context on
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:3960:    the first failure. Today's BULL_SILVER_X5_AVA_4 position was stuck
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-3961-    because ``get_stop_losses_strict`` raised Playwright "sync API
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-3962-    inside asyncio loop" during a fish-engine sell path. A stale
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-3963-    browser-context handle is the most common transient cause
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-3964-    (mirrors the TargetClosedError pattern fixed in commit aff3e90).
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-3965-    ``close_playwright()`` forces the singleton to re-launch on next
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-3966-    use, which rescues the retry without changing any contract. If
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-3967-    the retry also fails, fall through to the original fail-closed
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-3968-    path so the sell is still blocked rather than proceeding without
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-3969-    a rollback record.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-3970-
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-3971-    Returns ``(False, [])`` on read failure — fail closed: callers MUST
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-3972-    NOT proceed with the sell when the rollback record is unknown.
--

146-        }

148-    @classmethod
149-    def from_dict(cls, d: dict[str, Any]) -> "InstrumentState":
150-        return cls(
151-            ob_id=str(d["ob_id"]),
152-            ticker=str(d["ticker"]),
153-            cert_name=str(d.get("cert_name", "")),
154-            active_direction=d.get("active_direction"),
155-            buy_ladder=[TierOrder.from_dict(o) for o in d.get("buy_ladder", [])],
156-            sell_ladder=[TierOrder.from_dict(o) for o in d.get("sell_ladder", [])],
157-            stop_loss_id=d.get("stop_loss_id"),
158-            stop_loss_price=d.get("stop_loss_price"),
159-            inventory_units=int(d.get("inventory_units", 0) or 0),
160-            avg_entry_price=float(d.get("avg_entry_price", 0.0) or 0.0),
161-            session_pnl_sek=float(d.get("session_pnl_sek", 0.0) or 0.0),
162-            fills_this_session=int(d.get("fills_this_session", 0) or 0),
163-            consecutive_losses=int(d.get("consecutive_losses", 0) or 0),
164:            cooldown_until=d.get("cooldown_until"),
165:            last_direction_flip_ts=d.get("last_direction_flip_ts"),
166-        )
167-
168-    # ---- queries -----------------------------------------------------------
169-
170-    def armed_buy_tiers(self) -> list[TierOrder]:
171-        return [o for o in self.buy_ladder if o.status == ORDER_ARMED]
172-
173-    def armed_sell_tiers(self) -> list[TierOrder]:
174-        return [o for o in self.sell_ladder if o.status == ORDER_ARMED]
175-
176-    def planned_notional_sek(self) -> float:
177-        """SEK at risk = armed buys (notional) + inventory @ avg entry."""
178-        armed = sum(o.price * o.qty for o in self.armed_buy_tiers())
179-        held = self.inventory_units * self.avg_entry_price
180-        return armed + held
181-
182-    def hit_per_instrument_cap(self) -> bool:
183-        return self.planned_notional_sek() >= GRID_PER_INSTRUMENT_MAX_SEK
184-
185-    def session_loss_breached(self) -> bool:
186-        return self.session_pnl_sek <= -abs(GRID_PER_SESSION_LOSS_LIMIT_SEK)
187-
188-    def in_cooldown(self, now_iso: Optional[str] = None) -> bool:
189:        if not self.cooldown_until:
190-            return False
191-        now_iso = now_iso or _utcnow_iso()
192:        return now_iso < self.cooldown_until

456:def flip_direction(
457-    inst: InstrumentState,
458-    new_direction: str,
459-    *,
460:    cooldown_min: int = GRID_DIRECTION_FLIP_COOLDOWN_MIN,
461-) -> None:
462-    """Transition the instrument to a new direction with cooldown.
463-
464-    The existing inventory + sell ladder are NOT touched — they exit on
465-    their own. Only the buy ladder gets cleared; the caller is responsible
466-    for cancelling those orders against Avanza.
467-    """
468-    if new_direction not in ("LONG", "SHORT"):
469-        raise ValueError(f"direction must be LONG or SHORT, got {new_direction!r}")
470-    if inst.active_direction == new_direction:
471-        return
472-    inst.active_direction = new_direction
473:    inst.last_direction_flip_ts = _utcnow_iso()
474-    inst.buy_ladder = []  # caller cancels live orders before calling this
475-    cooldown_dt = _dt.datetime.now(_dt.timezone.utc) + _dt.timedelta(minutes=cooldown_min)
476:    inst.cooldown_until = cooldown_dt.strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")

561-def global_planned_notional(state: GridFisherState) -> float:
562-    """Sum planned notional across every instrument (armed buys +
563-    inventory at avg entry). Used by the global-cap gate."""
564-    return sum(inst.planned_notional_sek()

568-def should_halt_global(state: GridFisherState) -> Optional[str]:
569-    """Return a halt reason string if the global drawdown breaks a limit,
570:    else None. Caller flips ``state.halted = True`` and logs.
571-
572-    Currently uses session-wide P&L; richer drawdown tracking lives in
573-    ``risk_management.py`` and is wired in via a later batch.
574-    """
575-    # Per-session global loss limit = sum of per-instrument limits.
576-    threshold = -abs(GRID_PER_SESSION_LOSS_LIMIT_SEK) * max(
577-        len(state.by_instrument), 1
578-    )
579-    if state.global_session_pnl_sek <= threshold:
580-        return f"global_session_pnl<{threshold:.0f}sek"
581-    return None

Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_snipe_manager.py:425:        # SEK calculation downstream of compute_exit_plan wrong by ~10x.
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_snipe_manager.py:426:        # exit_optimizer.py:312 multiplies underlying USD by usdsek to get

Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_snipe_manager.py-429-        # magnitude. Fetch the live rate (with the same 15-min cache the
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_snipe_manager.py-430-        # rest of the system uses) and fall back to 10.85 only on total
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_snipe_manager.py-431-        # API failure (matching fx_rates' own fallback behavior).

Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_snipe_manager.py:433:        live_usdsek = fetch_usd_sek() or 10.85
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_snipe_manager.py-434-        # A-MC-4 (2026-04-11): Real entry timestamp instead of `now()`.
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_snipe_manager.py-435-        # Previously fin_snipe_manager always passed entry_ts=now() to the
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_snipe_manager.py-436-        # exit_optimizer, which made `hold_hours = (now - entry_ts) ≈ 0`
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_snipe_manager.py-437-        # for every cycle. The HOLD_TIME_EXTENDED risk flag at
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_snipe_manager.py-438-        # exit_optimizer.py:390-393 fires only when hold_hours > 5, so it
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_snipe_manager.py-439-        # never fired — the flag was permanently disabled despite being
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_snipe_manager.py-440-        # in the live code path.
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_snipe_manager.py-441-        #
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_snipe_manager.py-442-        # Fix: persist the entry timestamp in instrument_state on first
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_snipe_manager.py-443-        # observation of a non-zero position, then read it back on
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_snipe_manager.py-444-        # subsequent calls. Bootstrap behavior: existing positions get
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_snipe_manager.py-445-        # entry_ts = "first time we see them after this fix ships", which
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_snipe_manager.py-446-        # is acceptable — it just means HOLD_TIME_EXTENDED becomes
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_snipe_manager.py-447-        # available 5h after restart for already-open positions, vs.
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_snipe_manager.py-448-        # never. New positions get the real first-observation time.

Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-616-    """Deduplicate levels within 0.2% of each other and rank by EV."""
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-617-    sorted_results = sorted(results, key=lambda x: x[key_field], reverse=True)
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-618-    deduped: list[dict] = []
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-619-    for r in sorted_results:
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-620-        if not deduped or abs(r[key_field] - deduped[-1][key_field]) / r[key_field] > 0.002:
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-621-            deduped.append(r)
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-622-
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-623-    for r in deduped:
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py:624:        r["ev_score"] = round(r["fill_prob"] * r["bounce_to_spot_pct"], 4)
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-625-
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-626-    deduped.sort(key=lambda x: x["ev_score"], reverse=True)
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-627-    return deduped

Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-888-    machine-readable structure for the snipe manager.
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-889-
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-890-    Each plan dict::
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-891-

Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-894-            "spot": 69.21,
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-895-            "direction": "LONG",      # or "SHORT"

Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-897-            "levels": [...],           # fishing level dicts with fill_prob, ev_sek
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-898-            "instrument": {"ob_id": ..., "name": ..., "leverage": ...},
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-899-            "tp_cascade": [...],       # from FISHING_TP_CASCADE config
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-900-            "sl_cascade": [...],       # from FISHING_SL_CASCADE config
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-901-        }
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-902-    """
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-903-    if budget_sek is None:

Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-955-    """Format fishing plans into a concise Telegram message.

Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-960-        BULL X5: fish $67.50 (-2.5%) fill 11% EV 31
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-961-        BEAR X5: fish $71.00 (+2.6%) fill 8% EV 22

Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-963-    """

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-44-# 2026-04-09 Stage 1 log migration (docs/LOG_MIGRATION_AUDIT_20260409.md):
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-45-# The existing print-based `log()` / `_safe_print()` helpers were the only
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-46-# log output path in this process before 2026-04-09 afternoon. Fleet v1 added
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-47-# `logger` + 43 bare-except observability calls. This stage wires `log()`
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-48-# as a thin shim that delegates to `logger.info()` — all future 290+ call
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-49-# sites get timestamps + level prefix automatically, and the handful of
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:50:# logger.warning/error calls in the file format consistently.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-51-#
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-52-# Library discipline (codex adversarial review finding HIGH, 2026-04-09):
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-53-# IMPORTING this module must NOT install any handlers or touch the root
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:54:# logger. Doing so (a) silently disables parent-process telemetry —
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-55-# pytest caplog, file/structured handlers in embedding processes, etc. —
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-56-# or (b) causes duplicate output if the parent has its own handler. The
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-57-# correct pattern: libraries never configure handlers; the application
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-58-# entrypoint configures them at startup.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-59-#
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-60-# Handler installation happens only in `_install_stage1_logging()`, which
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-61-# is called from the `if __name__ == "__main__":` block at the bottom of
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-62-# this file (the real production entrypoint). When imported (pytest, other
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-63-# scripts, REPL), no handler is installed — callers that want to capture
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-64-# log output should use pytest's `caplog` with
--

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-100-        #    which would bypass our ASCII sanitization fallback.

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-496-# Mirror of the exit-side velocity alert but in the opposite direction and
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-497-# NOT gated on having an active position. When a breakout is detected we write
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-498-# a momentum candidate to ENTRY_MOMENTUM_STATE_FILE; metals_swing_trader reads
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-499-# this file and relaxes the usual MIN_BUY_CONFIDENCE/MIN_BUY_VOTERS gates when
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-500-# a fresh candidate matches the ticker and direction.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-501-#
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-502-# Motivation: the 2026-04-17 silver rip moved +1.3% in 20 min on volume ratio
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-503-# 2.2x while the swing trader's snapshot-based gates required
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-504-# MIN_BUY_CONFIDENCE (was 0.60, since 2026-05-04 0.56) AND 2-consecutive
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-505-# regime-confirm checks. Those thresholds are correct for range entries but
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:506:# silently reject breakout entries that move faster than the confirmation cadence.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-507-SILVER_ENTRY_FAST_TICK_ENABLED = True
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-508-SILVER_ENTRY_VELOCITY_WINDOW = 18          # 18 × 10s = 3 min (matches exit window)
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-509-SILVER_ENTRY_VELOCITY_ALERT_PCT = 0.8      # +% rise required over the window
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-510-SILVER_ENTRY_MIN_RVOL = 1.5                # require volume ratio ≥ this (vol confirm)
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-511-SILVER_ENTRY_DEDUP_WINDOW_SEC = 300        # one trigger per 5 min max
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-512-GOLD_ENTRY_FAST_TICK_ENABLED = True
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-513-GOLD_ENTRY_VELOCITY_WINDOW = 18            # same 3-min window
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-514-GOLD_ENTRY_VELOCITY_ALERT_PCT = 0.4        # gold's typical range is ~half of silver's
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-515-GOLD_ENTRY_MIN_RVOL = 1.5
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-516-GOLD_ENTRY_DEDUP_WINDOW_SEC = 300

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-832-# single-line change cannot reactivate a known-losing strategy.

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-835-

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-839-    If someone flips ``FISH_ENGINE_ENABLED = True`` without also removing
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-840-    this assertion and the call sites below, the first execution path
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:841:    raises loudly instead of silently activating a losing strategy.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-842-
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-843-    Kept as a function (not a module-level assert) so it is testable and
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-844-    so future operators who intentionally revive the engine have to make
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-845-    a deliberate, auditable change (delete the function AND remove the
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-846-    call at every use site). See the FISH_ENGINE_ENABLED declaration for
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-847-    the full revival checklist.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-848-    """

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:850:        raise RuntimeError(

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-852-            "declaration-site comment for the 2026-04-15 12,257 SEK loss "
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-853-            "context and revival requirements before re-enabling."
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-854-        )

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-856-

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-995-    # Codex review v6 finding MEDIUM (2026-04-09): `logger.hasHandlers()`

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-997-    # or an ERROR-level StreamHandler satisfies both checks but still

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1004-    # - pytest caplog.at_level('metals_loop', INFO): caplog adds a

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1006-    # - Library import, no setup: no handler → fallback → stdout ✓

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1028-    - Exit-side silver tick (``_silver_fast_tick``): fires only when a silver
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1029-      position is active. Runs the downside-velocity alerts and threshold
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py:1030:      warnings that protect an existing position.

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-1037-    """

Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-2700-    """Run one fish engine tick. Called every 60s by the main loop.
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-2701-
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-2702-    Builds a state dict from metals loop data, calls the engine,
Q:/finance-analyzer/data/metals_loop.py-2703-    and executes any buy/sell decisions via Avanza.

Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-1-"""Fin Fish: intraday dip/spike fishing planner for metals warrants.
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-2-
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-3-Computes optimal limit buy levels for metals warrants based on:
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-4-- Recent daily range patterns (how deep do dips / how high do spikes go?)
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-5-- ATR-based volatility and first-passage-time fill probabilities
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-6-- Structural support/resistance levels (Fibonacci, pivot, smart money)
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-7-- Chronos/model drift for directional bias
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-8-- RSI-based direction selection (BULL vs BEAR fishing)
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py:9:- Avanza warrant barrier safety checks
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-10-
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-11-Supports both BULL (buy dips) and BEAR (buy spikes) fishing.
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-12-
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-13-The output is a ranked table of fishing levels with fill probability,
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py:14:expected gain on bounce, EV in SEK, and barrier distance.
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-15-
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-16-Machine-readable output via ``compute_fishing_plan()`` for snipe manager
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-17-integration.  CLI one-shot via ``main()``.
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-18-"""
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-19-
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-20-from __future__ import annotations
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-21-
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-22-import argparse
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-23-import datetime
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-24-import logging

Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-711-    matching = _select_warrants(ticker, direction, spot)
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-712-
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-713-    results = []
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-714-    for warrant in matching:
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py:715:        barrier = warrant["barrier"]
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-716-        name = warrant["name"]
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py:717:        is_daily_cert = warrant["api_type"] == "certificate" and barrier == 0
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-718-
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py:719:        # Dynamic leverage: compute from spot and barrier for warrants.
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-720-        # Config leverage is stale (set when cert was added, not at current price).
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py:721:        # Daily certs (no barrier) keep config leverage.
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py:722:        if not is_daily_cert and barrier > 0:
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py:723:            dist = abs(spot - barrier)
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-724-            leverage = spot / dist if dist > 0 else warrant["leverage"]
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-725-        else:
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-726-            leverage = warrant["leverage"]
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-727-
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py:728:        # Barrier checks only for MINI warrants (barrier > 0)
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py:729:        if not is_daily_cert and barrier > 0:
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py:730:            if direction == "LONG" and spot <= barrier:
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-731-                continue  # knocked out
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py:732:            if direction == "SHORT" and spot >= barrier:
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py:733:                # BEAR MINIs get knocked out if underlying goes above barrier
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-734-                # (depends on product, but skip if too close)
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-735-                pass
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py:736:            barrier_distance = abs(spot - barrier) / spot * 100
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py:737:            if barrier_distance < MIN_BARRIER_DISTANCE_PCT:
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-738-                continue
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-739-        else:
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py:740:            barrier_distance = 100.0
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-741-
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-742-        for fl in fishing_levels:
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-743-            level = fl["level"]
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-744-
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py:745:            # Check barrier safety at fishing level (MINI warrants only)
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py:746:            if not is_daily_cert and barrier > 0:
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-747-                if direction == "LONG":
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py:748:                    fish_barrier_dist = round((level - barrier) / level * 100, 2)
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py-749-                else:
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py:750:                    fish_barrier_dist = round(abs(level - barrier) / level * 100, 2)
Q:/finance-analyzer/portfolio/fin_fish.py:751:                if fish_barrier_dist < MIN_BARRIER_DISTANCE_PCT:

44-# 2026-04-09 Stage 1 log migration (docs/LOG_MIGRATION_AUDIT_20260409.md):
45-# The existing print-based `log()` / `_safe_print()` helpers were the only
46-# log output path in this process before 2026-04-09 afternoon. Fleet v1 added
47-# `logger` + 43 bare-except observability calls. This stage wires `log()`
48-# as a thin shim that delegates to `logger.info()` — all future 290+ call
49-# sites get timestamps + level prefix automatically, and the handful of
50-# logger.warning/error calls in the file format consistently.
51-#
52-# Library discipline (codex adversarial review finding HIGH, 2026-04-09):
53-# IMPORTING this module must NOT install any handlers or touch the root
54:# logger. Doing so (a) silently disables parent-process telemetry —
55-# pytest caplog, file/structured handlers in embedding processes, etc. —
56-# or (b) causes duplicate output if the parent has its own handler. The
57-# correct pattern: libraries never configure handlers; the application
58-# entrypoint configures them at startup.
59-#
60-# Handler installation happens only in `_install_stage1_logging()`, which
61-# is called from the `if __name__ == "__main__":` block at the bottom of
62-# this file (the real production entrypoint). When imported (pytest, other
63-# scripts, REPL), no handler is installed — callers that want to capture
64-# log output should use pytest's `caplog` with
--
96-        self.stream = sys.stdout
97-        # 2. Write directly (NOT via super().emit()) so we can catch
98-        #    UnicodeEncodeError ourselves. super().emit() has its own
99-        #    try/except that routes exceptions through self.handleError,
100-        #    which would bypass our ASCII sanitization fallback.
101-        try:
102-            msg = self.format(record)
103-            self.stream.write(msg + self.terminator)
104-            self.flush()
105-        except UnicodeEncodeError:
106:            # Sanitize the formatted message to ASCII-replace and retry.
107-            # Same idea as the old `_safe_print` fallback, now integrated
108-            # into the logging path so every logger.* call benefits.
109-            try:
110-                safe = msg.encode("ascii", "replace").decode("ascii")
111-                self.stream.write(safe + self.terminator)
112-                self.flush()
113-            except Exception:
114-                self.handleError(record)
115-        except Exception:
116-            self.handleError(record)

161-def _has_ancestor_emitter(lg: logging.Logger, target_level: int) -> bool:
162-    """Walk the logger hierarchy to find a handler that would emit `target_level`.
163-
164-    Used by log() and metals_swing_trader._log() to decide whether
165-    logger.info() will actually produce visible output, or whether to
166-    fall back to a direct stdout print.
167-
168-    Codex review v6 finding MEDIUM (2026-04-09): the naive check
169-    `logger.hasHandlers() and isEnabledFor(INFO)` passes for an ancestor
170-    NullHandler or an ERROR-level StreamHandler — both scenarios drop
171:    INFO records silently. This walk handles:
172-
173-    * NullHandler — skipped (it's a no-op absorbing handler)
174-    * Level-filtered handlers — only returns True if level permits target
175-    * propagate=False — stops walking when an ancestor blocks propagation
176-
177-    Returns True only when we're confident an INFO record will actually

496-# Mirror of the exit-side velocity alert but in the opposite direction and
497-# NOT gated on having an active position. When a breakout is detected we write
498-# a momentum candidate to ENTRY_MOMENTUM_STATE_FILE; metals_swing_trader reads
499-# this file and relaxes the usual MIN_BUY_CONFIDENCE/MIN_BUY_VOTERS gates when
500-# a fresh candidate matches the ticker and direction.
501-#
502-# Motivation: the 2026-04-17 silver rip moved +1.3% in 20 min on volume ratio
503-# 2.2x while the swing trader's snapshot-based gates required
504-# MIN_BUY_CONFIDENCE (was 0.60, since 2026-05-04 0.56) AND 2-consecutive
505-# regime-confirm checks. Those thresholds are correct for range entries but
506:# silently reject breakout entries that move faster than the confirmation cadence.
507-SILVER_ENTRY_FAST_TICK_ENABLED = True
508-SILVER_ENTRY_VELOCITY_WINDOW = 18          # 18 × 10s = 3 min (matches exit window)
509-SILVER_ENTRY_VELOCITY_ALERT_PCT = 0.8      # +% rise required over the window
510-SILVER_ENTRY_MIN_RVOL = 1.5                # require volume ratio ≥ this (vol confirm)
511-SILVER_ENTRY_DEDUP_WINDOW_SEC = 300        # one trigger per 5 min max
512-GOLD_ENTRY_FAST_TICK_ENABLED = True
513-GOLD_ENTRY_VELOCITY_WINDOW = 18            # same 3-min window
514-GOLD_ENTRY_VELOCITY_ALERT_PCT = 0.4        # gold's typical range is ~half of silver's
515-GOLD_ENTRY_MIN_RVOL = 1.5
516-GOLD_ENTRY_DEDUP_WINDOW_SEC = 300

988-def log(msg):
989-    # 2026-04-09 Stage 1 shim: delegate to logger.info when we can prove
990-    # an INFO-permissive, non-null handler exists in the logger hierarchy.
991-    # Otherwise fall back to direct stdout via _safe_print so the old
992-    # unconditional-stdout contract is preserved for library/programmatic
993-    # use.
994-    #
995-    # Codex review v6 finding MEDIUM (2026-04-09): `logger.hasHandlers()`
996-    # + `isEnabledFor(INFO)` is insufficient — an ancestor NullHandler
997-    # or an ERROR-level StreamHandler satisfies both checks but still
998:    # silently drops INFO records. `_has_ancestor_emitter()` walks
999-    # handlers and their levels (and respects propagate=False) to
1000-    # confirm at least one handler will actually emit this record.
1001-    #
1002-    # Branch truth table:
1003-    # - Production __main__ (handler installed, level=INFO): logger ✓
1004-    # - pytest caplog.at_level('metals_loop', INFO): caplog adds a
1005-    #   LogCaptureHandler to metals_loop → logger path ✓
1006-    # - Library import, no setup: no handler → fallback → stdout ✓
1007-    # - External NullHandler on root: skipped → no emitter → fallback ✓
1008-    # - External root StreamHandler(ERROR): level too high → fallback ✓

1212-        bars = r.json()
